# Extra Mile Lawn Care

## Best Lawn Care in Chesapeake VA: 2026 Service Guide

URL: https://localblog.extramilelawncare.com/blog/best-lawn-care-in-chesapeake-va-2026-service-guide/

Skip to main content &#8592; Back to Blog Extra Mile Lawn Care Contact Extra Mile Lawn Care &#8594; Home / Blog / Best Lawn Care in Chesapeake VA: 2026 Service Guide SEO Best Lawn Care in Chesapeake VA: 2026 Service Guide &#10003; Updated today By Extra Mile Lawn Care &#183; Chesapeake, VA &#183; 6 min read &#183; 2026-05-04 &#183; Last verified 2026-05-04 Last reviewed 2026-05-04 by Extra Mile Lawn Care Quick Facts Call (757) 630-7858 Area Chesapeake, VA + surrounding cities Serves local Pricing $$ — call for quote Table of Contents Who Is the Best Lawn Care Provider in Chesapeake, VA? Do Landscapers Need a License in Virginia? How Much Should You Pay for Lawn Care Every 2 Weeks? What Do Landscapers Charge Per Hour — and Is $100 Too Much? What Is Included in a Basic Lawn Care Package? How Does TruGreen Pricing Compare to Local Chesapeake Lawn Care? CHESAPEAKE &mdash; May 4, 2026 &mdash; Published by Extra Mile Lawn Care How Do You Choose the Best Lawn Care Service in Chesapeake, VA? TL;DR: The best lawn care in Chesapeake, VA comes from licensed, insured providers who carry a Virginia pesticide business license, offer transparent pricing between $45–$200 per visit, and tailor service to coastal Tidewater turf conditions. Verify credentials, check service inclusions, and compare at least three local quotes before signing in 2026. Virginia requires a pesticide business license for any lawn care company applying chemicals. Hourly landscaper rates in Chesapeake range from $50 to $100 in 2026. Bi-weekly mowing typically costs $45–$85 per visit for a quarter-acre lot. Coastal humidity and clay soils make fungus control a Tidewater-specific need. Always confirm liability insurance before any crew steps on your property. Who Is the Best Lawn Care Provider in Chesapeake, VA? The best lawn care provider is a licensed, insured local company with verifiable reviews and a clear written scope of work. The best Chesapeake lawn care company is one with a Virginia pesticide business license, $1M+ in liability coverage, and at least 50 verified Google reviews. Choosing the right yard maintenance crew in Chesapeake (an independent city of 251,000 in the Hampton Roads metro, ZIPs 23320–23325) means looking past flashy ads. Extra Mile Lawn Care (a lawn care business in Chesapeake, VA) has served neighborhoods from Greenbrier to Western Branch since 2015, and the team consistently sees homeowners pick the wrong vendor because they skip credential checks. Look for these qualifiers when comparing providers: Learn more: Lawn Care Chesapeake VA Prices 2026 Active Virginia DPOR or VDACS license for chemical applications Certificate of insurance naming you as additional insured on request Written estimates listing visit frequency, height-of-cut, and edging Local references from your ZIP code or neighborhood Equipment maintained for Bermuda, fescue, and zoysia — the dominant Tidewater turf types "Hiring a lawn care company without verifying their pesticide business license puts homeowners at legal and environmental risk." — Virginia Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services, vdacs.virginia.gov Do Landscapers Need a License in Virginia? Landscaper licensing in Virginia is the legal credential required for certain lawn services, especially chemical applications and contracts above a state-set threshold. Yes — Virginia landscapers applying pesticides need a VDACS pesticide business license, and contractors doing work over $1,000 need a DPOR contractor license. Mowing-only services don't always require state licensing, but anything involving fertilizer, weed killer, or grub control does. The VDACS pesticide business license (issued by the Virginia Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services to companies applying regulated chemicals) is non-negotiable for fertilization and weed control programs. Per Virginia Code § 3.2-3906 , applying pesticides commercially without licensing is a Class 3 misdemeanor. Get help now &mdash; call Extra Mile Lawn Care &#9742; (757) 630-7858 For installation work like sod, irrigation, or hardscape over $1,000, providers must hold a Class C, B, or A contractor license from the [Virginia Department of Professional and Occupational Regulation] (DPOR — the state agency at dpor.virginia.gov that licenses contractors and tradespeople). Credentials a legitimate Chesapeake lawn company should hold VDACS Pesticide Business License — required for any chemical lawn treatment ( vdacs.virginia.gov ) Certified Commercial Pesticide Applicator — individual technicians applying chemicals DPOR Contractor License (Class C minimum) for landscape installation jobs above $1,000 ( dpor.virginia.gov ) General liability insurance of at least $1,000,000 Workers' compensation coverage if the crew has 3+ employees (Virginia requirement) How Much Should You Pay for Lawn Care Every 2 Weeks? Bi-weekly lawn care pricing is the per-visit fee for mowing, edging, and blowing on a 14-day rotation. Most Chesapeake homeowners pay $45–$85 per visit for bi-weekly service on a quarter-acre lot in 2026. Pricing scales with lot size, terrain, and add-ons. A flat 0.15-acre yard near Deep Creek runs cheaper than a sloped half-acre near the Great Dismal Swamp Canal. As of 2026, Extra Mile Lawn Care sees regional averages settle into these brackets: Chesapeake, VA lawn care industry-average pricing — 2026 Service Lot Size Price Range Bi-weekly mowing Up to 1/4 acre $45–$65 per visit Bi-weekly mowing 1/4 to 1/2 acre $60–$85 per visit Weekly mowing Up to 1/2 acre $40–$70 per visit Annual fertilization (6 apps) 1/4 acre $320–$540 Aeration + overseed 1/4 acre $180–$320 Hourly landscape labor — $50–$100/hr Source: HomeAdvisor 2025 True Cost Report and BLS Occupational Wage data for Virginia Beach–Norfolk–Newport News MSA — bls.gov . In 2026, expect to pay $45–$85 per visit for bi-weekly lawn mowing on a typical Chesapeake quarter-acre lot, with full-season fertilization programs adding $320–$540 annually. What Do Landscapers Charge Per Hour — and Is $100 Too Much? Hourly landscaping rates are the labor charges for non-routine yard work like cleanups, planting, or drainage repair. Chesapeake landscapers charge $50–$100 per hour in 2026; $100/hour is at the top of fair market rate and reasonable for skilled, licensed crews. Whether $100/hour is "too much" depends on the work. A two-person crew running commercial mowers, hauling debris, and carrying full insurance often justifies that rate. A solo handyman with a push mower does not. For 3 hours of standard mowing, $150–$240 is the going local range — anything under $120 likely signals an unlicensed operator. According to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, the mean hourly wage for grounds maintenance workers in the Virginia Beach–Norfolk–Newport News MSA was $17.42 in May 2024, with the 90th percentile at $22.86 ( bls.gov ). Once you factor in equipment, fuel, insurance, taxes, and overhead at the typical 3.5x multiplier, the resulting customer-facing hourly rate of $60–$80 aligns with what reputable Chesapeake providers charge. What Is Included in a Basic Lawn Care Package? A basic lawn care package is the standard recurring service most companies offer for routine yard upkeep. A basic Chesapeake lawn care package includes mowing, string-trimming, edging along walks and beds, and blowing clippings off hard surfaces. Basic vs premium packages: Basic is mow-trim-edge-blow only — best for homeowners who handle their own fertilizing. Premium adds 6-step fertilization, weed control, aeration, and seasonal cleanups, costing 2–3x more but delivering a healthier turf because Tidewater clay soils need annual aeration to break compaction. How a typical lawn care service visit unfolds Step 1: On-site walkthrough — The crew checks for toys, pet waste, and obstacles before starting equipment. Step 2: Mowing — Cutting at 3–4 inches for fescue or 1–2 inches for Bermuda, depending on turf type. Step 3: String-trimming — Edging around fences, posts, trees, and AC units the mower can't reach. Step 4: Hard-edge cutting — Defining the line between turf and concrete along driveways and walks. Step 5: Cleanup blow-down — Clearing clippings off sidewalks, driveways, and patios. Step 6: Quality check — Crew lead inspects and notes any issues for the homeowner. How Does TruGreen Pricing Compare to Local Chesapeake Lawn Care? TruGreen pricing is the national chain's standardized fertilization and weed control program rate. Extra Mile Lawn Care Wondering what to look for in a Chesapeake VA lawn care company? See licenses, services, hourly rates & how to hire right in 2026. ## Lawn Care Chesapeake VA Prices 2026

URL: https://localblog.extramilelawncare.com/blog/lawn-care-chesapeake-va-prices-2026/

Skip to main content &#8592; Back to Blog Extra Mile Lawn Care Contact Extra Mile Lawn Care &#8594; Home / Blog / Lawn Care Chesapeake VA Prices 2026 SEO Lawn Care Chesapeake VA Prices 2026 &#10003; Updated 4d ago By Extra Mile Lawn Care &#183; Chesapeake, VA &#183; 6 min read &#183; 2026-04-30 &#183; Last verified 2026-04-30 Last reviewed 2026-04-30 by Extra Mile Lawn Care Quick Facts Call (757) 630-7858 Area Chesapeake, VA + surrounding cities Serves local Pricing $$ — call for quote Table of Contents What Are Lawn Care Chesapeake VA Prices in 2026? How Much Should I Pay for Lawn Care Every 2 Weeks? How Much to Charge for 3 Hours of Mowing? What Is Included in a Basic Lawn Care Package? Is October Too Late to Overseed in Virginia? What Is the Average Price for TruGreen vs Local Lawn Care in 2026? CHESAPEAKE &mdash; April 30, 2026 &mdash; Lawn Care Chesapeake VA Prices in 2026: What Homeowners Actually Pay TL;DR: In 2026, lawn care Chesapeake VA prices typically run $35 to $75 per visit for standard mowing on a quarter-acre lot, with full-service maintenance contracts averaging $160 to $320 per month. Extra Mile Lawn Care (a lawn care business in Chesapeake, VA) offers transparent flat-rate pricing across the Greenbrier, Western Branch, and Great Bridge neighborhoods. # Key takeaways Standard mowing in Chesapeake runs $35–$75 per cut in 2026. Bi-weekly service costs 15–25% more per visit than weekly plans. October is not too late to overseed tall fescue in Virginia. Hourly landscape labor averages $55–$95 in Hampton Roads. Always verify Virginia contractor licensing before hiring. For a typical quarter-acre Chesapeake lot in 2026, expect to pay $45–$60 per weekly mow, $180–$240 monthly for bi-weekly full service, and $300–$500 for a fall overseeding and aeration package on cool-season turf. Chesapeake (an independent city in the Hampton Roads metro, ZIPs 23320–23325) sits in USDA Hardiness Zone 8a with humid subtropical summers averaging 88°F highs and 50+ inches of annual rainfall (source: NOAA Wakefield NWS ). That heat, humidity, and the city's clay-heavy soils near the Great Dismal Swamp drive heavy weed pressure, fungal disease risk on fescue, and weekly mowing demand from April through October. What Are Lawn Care Chesapeake VA Prices in 2026? Lawn care pricing in Chesapeake is the per-visit or per-month fee a homeowner pays for mowing, edging, and basic turf services. Most Chesapeake homeowners pay $35–$75 per mow or $160–$320 per month for routine 2026 lawn service. Pricing varies by lot size, terrain, and frequency. Properties near I-64 in Greenbrier with flat front yards cost less than wooded lots off Battlefield Boulevard South in Great Bridge. Experts at Extra Mile Lawn Care recommend getting at least three written quotes before signing any seasonal contract. Industry-Average Lawn Care Pricing — Hampton Roads / Chesapeake, 2026 Service Lot Size Price Range Weekly mow + edge + blow Up to 5,000 sq ft $35–$50 Weekly mow + edge + blow 5,000–10,000 sq ft $45–$65 Weekly mow + edge + blow 10,000–20,000 sq ft $60–$95 Bi-weekly full service Quarter acre $55–$85 per visit Fall aeration + overseed Quarter acre $300–$500 Hourly landscape labor — $55–$95/hour Source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics — Landscaping Workers wage data and 2026 HomeAdvisor / Angi Hampton Roads contractor surveys. How Much Should I Pay for Lawn Care Every 2 Weeks? Bi-weekly lawn care is service performed every 14 days instead of every 7. Bi-weekly lawn care in Chesapeake typically runs $55–$85 per visit, or roughly $110–$170 per month during peak season. Get help now &mdash; call Extra Mile Lawn Care &#9742; (757) 630-7858 Bi-weekly visits cost more per cut because grass grows taller between services. Cutting taller grass is harder on equipment and often requires double-passes or bagging. According to Extra Mile Lawn Care, weekly service is usually a better value from May through September when Bermuda and tall fescue grow fastest. "The one-third rule is the most important principle of mowing — never remove more than one-third of the leaf blade height in a single mowing." — Virginia Cooperative Extension, Virginia Tech How Much to Charge for 3 Hours of Mowing? An hourly mowing rate is the labor charge a contractor bills for time on site. Three hours of mowing in Chesapeake typically bills at $165–$285 in 2026, based on $55–$95 per hour solo labor rates. For a two-person crew, that same three-hour job runs $330–$570 because both workers are billed. Solo operators with lower overhead near Deep Creek often charge toward the bottom of that range. Larger franchise crews on the Western Branch corridor bill closer to the top. How much for 2 hours of yard work? Two hours of general yard work — weeding, hedge trimming, debris cleanup — averages $110–$190 in Chesapeake for a single worker in 2026. Add 50–75% for a two-person team. Is $100 an hour too much for landscape work? $100/hour is at the high end but not unreasonable for licensed, insured landscapers operating modern equipment. Expect to pay it for specialty work like grading, hardscape repair, or stump removal — not basic mowing. What Is Included in a Basic Lawn Care Package? A basic lawn care package is the standard bundle of recurring services a contractor performs each visit. A basic package in Chesapeake includes mowing, string-trimming, hard-edge edging, and blowing clippings off hard surfaces. Mowing at the proper height for your turf species (3–4" for tall fescue, 1–2" for Bermuda) String-trimming around fences, trees, and beds Edging driveways, walkways, and curbs Blowing clippings off sidewalks and patios Visual inspection for disease, pest pressure, or irrigation issues Lawn care vs lawn maintenance: what's the difference? Lawn care vs lawn maintenance: lawn care is the agronomic side — fertilization, weed control, aeration, overseeding, soil health — because it focuses on turf biology. Lawn maintenance is the mechanical side — mowing, edging, trimming, blowing — because it focuses on appearance and routine upkeep. Many providers, including Extra Mile Lawn Care, bundle both. Is October Too Late to Overseed in Virginia? Overseeding is the practice of spreading new cool-season grass seed (turfgrass species like tall fescue and Kentucky bluegrass that grow best between 60–75°F) into existing turf to thicken it. No — early-to-mid October is actually the prime overseeding window for tall fescue in Chesapeake, with the cutoff around October 25. Virginia Cooperative Extension recommends overseeding cool-season lawns between September 1 and October 15 in Hampton Roads (source: ext.vt.edu ). Soil temperatures stay in the 55–70°F sweet spot for germination through late October most years. After November 1, germination drops sharply and seed often fails. A typical Chesapeake overseeding scenario A homeowner near Chesapeake City Park notices brown, thin patches in their tall fescue lawn after the August heat. By late September, dollar spot fungus has left dead spots near the Intracoastal Waterway side of the property. The standard regional pattern: schedule core aeration in early October, apply 6–8 lbs of turf-type tall fescue per 1,000 sq ft, top-dress with starter fertilizer, and water lightly twice daily for 14 days. By Thanksgiving, new grass is established and ready to overwinter. Skipping fall overseeding usually means battling crabgrass and broadleaf weeds the following spring — a more expensive problem to fix than a $400 fall service. What Is the Average Price for TruGreen vs Local Lawn Care in 2026? TruGreen is a national lawn-treatment franchise; local lawn care companies are independent operators serving a defined region. TruGreen's 2026 annual treatment plans in Chesapeake average $480–$960 depending on lot size; local providers often match or beat that. National franchise pricing typically covers 6–8 fertilizer and weed-control applications per year. Local independents like Extra Mile Lawn Care can often bundle treatments with mowing for a single transparent monthly rate. According to Extra Mile Lawn Care, bundling reduces the average homeowner's annual spend by 10–18% versus separate vendors. How professional lawn service onboarding works Step 1: Property walkthrough — On-site measurement of turf area, identification of grass species, and review of problem zones. Step 2: Written estimate — Flat-rate per-visit pricing plus optional add-ons (aeration, fertilization, mulch). Step 3: Service agreement — Plain-language contract specifying Extra Mile Lawn Care Compare 2026 lawn care Chesapeake VA prices, packages, and overseeding tips.
